zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Braose於1305年出生在英國戴文斯托肯哈姆時，她的父親Sir Peter de Braose33歲，母親Agnes de Clifford29歲。她於大約1328年與Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1349年享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Braose于1305年出生在英国戴文斯托肯哈姆时，她的父亲Sir Peter de Braose33岁，母亲Agnes de Clifford29岁。她于大约1328年与Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk在英国肯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1349年享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Braose was born in 1305, in Stokenham, Devon, England, her father, Sir Peter de Braose, was 33 and her mother, Agnes de Clifford, was 29. She married Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk about 1328, in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1349, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Braose est née en 1305, à Stokenham, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Peter de Braose, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Clifford, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk environ 1328, à Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1349, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Braose 1305 in Stokenham, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Peter de Braose 33 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Clifford 29. Sie hat um 1328 in Kent, England Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1349 im Alter von 44 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Braose è nata il nel 1305 nel Stokenham, Devon, England, sua padre, Sir Peter de Braose, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Clifford, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk circa 1328, nel Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1349, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Braoseが1305年にイギリスデボンストケンハムで生まれた時，父Sir Peter de Braoseは33歳，母Agnes de Cliffordは29歳であった。約1328年にイギリスケントでThomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1349年に44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Braose(이)가 1305년 영국 데폰 스토켄함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Peter de Braose(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Clifford(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 대략 1328년 영국 켄트에서 Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Braose nasceu em 1305, em Stokenham, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Peter de Braose, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Clifford, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk aproximadamente 1328, em Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1349, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Braose родилась в 1305 г. в местности стокенам, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Peter de Braose было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Clifford, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk около 1328 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1349 г. в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Braose nació en 1305, en Stokenham, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Peter de Braose, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Agnes de Clifford, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk aproximadamente 1328, en Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1349, a la edad de 44 años.
